---
title: "PyCryption Lab"
subtitle: "Specimens, composers, and benchmarks for encryption research"
toc: false
---

Welcome to the front desk. PyCryption is an encryption research lab built
around **composers** — harnesses that wrap encryption algorithms with
automatic metrics collection, so testing an idea costs minutes instead of an
afternoon of scaffolding.

## The Wings

:::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .wing-card}
### {{< fa road >}} [On-Ramp](General%20Cryptography.ipynb)
Cryptography fundamentals, level by level — from XOR to KEMs. The
mathematics track and exercises will grow here.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .wing-card}
### {{< fa flask >}} [Composer Tutorial](Encryption%20Composer.ipynb)
Build, test, and benchmark a prototype algorithm against production AEADs
in a few decorated lines.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .wing-card}
### {{< fa shield-halved >}} [ML-KEM (Post-Quantum)](ML-KEM.ipynb)
ML-KEM-768 + AES-256-GCM hybrid (FIPS 203) — sender/recipient roles and
what the per-message KEM tax actually costs.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .wing-card}
### {{< fa chart-line >}} [Benchmarks](benchmarks.qmd)
Longitudinal, commit-stamped measurements — seeded payloads, machine
fingerprints, and throughput trends across runs.
:::

::::

## The Specimen Shelf

Production-grade implementations in `lib/algorithms`, all rideable through
the composer:

| Specimen | Class | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **AES-256-GCM** | block-cipher AEAD | the workhorse |
| **ChaCha20-Poly1305** | stream AEAD (RFC 8439) | Salsa20's modern descendant |
| **ML-KEM-768 + AES-256-GCM** | post-quantum KEM-DEM | fresh encapsulation per message |
| **PQ-Wrap [AES → ML-KEM]** | layered pipeline | classical + PQ defense in depth |

Layering is first-class: `MultiEncryption` chains any algorithms and reports
each layer's cost, and a pipeline registers in a `ComposerSession` like any
single specimen — benchmarks, quality analysis, and all.

## Running the Lab

```bash
uv sync         # install dependencies
task            # list all lab operations
task test       # full test suite
task ride       # benchmark the specimen shelf in your terminal
task analyze    # output quality panel: entropy, avalanche, ECB canary
task bench:save # persist a seeded, commit-stamped benchmark run
```

Every result table on this site comes from committed notebook outputs or
persisted benchmark records — the site build never executes crypto. Outputs
refresh deliberately via `task nb:exec`, so benchmarks are reviewable in git
history like any other change.
